title: Jim Baikie

{{Quote|Jim Baikie was a wonderful artist, his talents forged during a wonderful period, and throughout his long career his work sang with the zealous energy and unrestrained inventiveness of those times. He was a great talent, a great collaborator and a great friend. I'd like to send all my love to Wendy and to Jim’s family. He was a dear, astonishing man, and I'll remember him always.|Alan Moore|<ref name="Alan Moore tribute">{{WebCite|url=https://downthetubes.net/jim-baikie-an-appreciation-by-alan-moore/|text=Jim Baikie: An Appreciation by Alan Moore|work=Down the Tubes|author=[[Alan Moore]]|date=2018-01-16|archivedate=20250421170614}}</ref>}}
'''Jim Baikie''' ([[February 28]], [[1940s#1940|1940]]–[[December 29]], [[2017]]) was a [[Wikipedia:Scottish people|Scottish]] artist who did the inking, penciling, and coloring for ''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]'' for [[Dark Horse Comics]].

Baikie was most famous for working on ''[[Wikipedia:Skizz|Skizz]]'' with [[Alan Moore]]. He began his career in the [[1960s]] working for several British girls' and tv-related comics, and he was a comics artists for four decades. Baikie received an [[Wikipedia:Eisner Awards|Eisner Award]] in [[2000]].<ref name="Bio">{{WebCite|url=https://www.lambiek.net/artists/b/baikie_jim.htm|text=Jim Baikie|work=Lambiek Comiclopedia|archivedate=20260112043448}}</ref>
